tekst: That jingle is absolutely illegal — in the best possible way. Welcome back to Piggy's World Cup podcast, I'm Frank!
And I'm Mitch! It is June fourteenth, the World Cup is well and truly underway, and oh my days — what a day of football we just had.

Let's get straight into the results. USA four, Paraguay one. Absolute demolition job in Los Angeles. Folarin Balogun with two goals — the Americans are electric, and the whole country is losing its mind.
And apparently a ten-year-old Norwegian kid named Tius from Drammen performed at the opening ceremony with Katy Perry! Only at a World Cup, Frank. Only at a World Cup.

Then Germany — seven, one against Curaçao. Seven! And listen, credit to Curaçao — twenty-five of their twenty-six players were born outside the country. Incredible story just making it there, but yeah, seven-one is a rough welcome to the tournament.

Kai Havertz grabbed two goals, joins Balogun at the top of the scoring charts. Meanwhile Brazil drew one-one with Morocco — halftime I thought Brazil were going to run riot, but no. And honestly, the headlines about half of Brazil not even caring about this World Cup anymore? That's heartbreaking stuff.

Qatar drew one-one with Switzerland in injury time — their captain, thirty-five years old, heads in a late equalizer. People are comparing it to Kjetil Rekdal against Brazil in ninety-eight! Big claim, but the drama was real. Scotland beat Haiti one-nil, Australia beat Turkey two-nil. Solid day for the underdogs.

Now — Piggy's competition standings! Simon is out in front with six points. Fair play, Simon, you smug genius. Then a four-way tie for second on five points — André, Marius, Jonas and Bjørn all breathing down his neck.

Ringstad and Tobias on four points, and Håkon bringing up the rear also on four. Håkon, mate, you predicted Brazil would walk over Morocco didn't you? That one-one is not doing your weekend any favors.
And Bjørn had Germany winning big — he's probably the happiest man alive right now after that seven-one. Maybe he predicted the exact score, knowing Bjørn he absolutely did not, but let him enjoy it.

Now, looking ahead — we've got Ivory Coast versus Ecuador in Group E, Sweden versus Tunisia in Group F, and the big one — Spain versus Cape Verde in Group H. Spain are currently topping Group H, so don't expect any surprises there, but Cape Verde will make it interesting.

Belgium versus Egypt in Group G — Belgium are leading that group too. And Saudi Arabia versus Uruguay in Group H, which is a genuinely spicy fixture. Uruguay have the quality but Saudi Arabia at a World Cup is never a free three points, ask Argentina about that.

On the Norway front — Solbakken is getting some protection from the press before the World Cup opener against Iraq, and Brede Hangeland is apparently running interference for him in the media. One journalist wrote that if Haaland's shot had hit the keeper, the keeper would have been dead. I mean — that's the endorsement of the century.

Classic Erling. Right, who's feeling bold for the upcoming matches? Marius has Sweden winning big and I respect that energy. Ringstad has apparently gone for a Belgium landslide against Egypt — could be the move that drags him back into contention.

Simon just needs to stay calm, not overthink it, and he'll cruise. But this competition is so tight — one bad round and you're last. Jonas, André, Bjørn — all right there. This is anyone's tournament, and so is the football itself.
Absolutely. Keep your predictions sharp, keep your rivalries sharper, and we'll be back to tear everything apart again very soon. For Frank, I'm Mitch — Hit the outro!
